sticky.description: Oil curve tightness remains visible across the M1-M6 window.
Freight markets are transmitting the shipping shock.
Middle East export grades are dislocating versus WTI.
Oman is richening versus U.S. crude, confirming regional dislocation.
Murban is richening versus U.S. crude, showing Gulf export tightness.
PortWatch shows live tanker transit deterioration through the Strait of Hormuz; this is the lead physical flow signal.
Global Fishing Watch provides secondary public AIS confirmation that port activity is deteriorating around the regional shipping network.
SEC and public-source insurance stress confirm the disruption is being acknowledged in structured disclosures and market commentary.
Spot oil remains elevated.
Funding stress is visible but still contained.
Consumer balance sheets are deteriorating.
Inflation compensation is creeping higher.
Treasury demand is softer but not yet broken.
Weak 5Y/7Y coupon clearing suggests Treasury stress is migrating inward before the long end fully breaks.
Rates volatility remains above calm levels.
Repo plumbing is functional.
Wage growth remains sticky enough to keep inflation persistence alive.
The payroll-tax base is softening only gradually, keeping nominal demand alive.
Direct FX spot and short-rate differentials show broad dollar funding pressure, but not a full break.
Official P&I notices show insurance withdrawal or reinstatement stress remains active.
IAEA verification ambiguity is keeping the oil shock structurally alive.
Medium-term forward inflation expectations are no longer mean-reverting cleanly.
The expected-inflation curve is lifting across maturities.
Combined market and survey expectations point to persistence rather than transitory repricing.
Survey expectations are rising faster than the market-implied short end.
Local-currency oil stress in major importer regions is feeding imported inflation and current-account strain.
World Bank structural importer vulnerability suggests the external shock is landing on balance sheets that were already exposed.
Real household purchasing power is being squeezed before labor fully rolls over.
Equity volatility is rising, hinting that the nominal shock is starting to tighten financial conditions.
IEA reserve-cover deterioration suggests the physical oil shock can persist instead of mean-reverting quickly.
Reserve-cover deterioration across key importers is raising the odds of imported inflation persistence.
A larger external liability burden makes Treasury demand more sensitive to a sustained external shock.
Weaker liability-side financing support implies less structural foreign sponsorship of U.S. duration.
Foreign-duration sponsorship is softening, but not yet in outright break territory.

convex.description: Brent M1-M6 backwardation is widening.
Freight costs are accelerating.
Regional crude spreads are broadening as the shock migrates.
Oman versus WTI is widening, reinforcing the Gulf supply shock.
Murban versus WTI is widening, showing cross-basin dislocation.
Hormuz tanker transit stress confirms the shipping shock is constraining flows.
Global Fishing Watch port-visit deterioration is secondary confirmation that the disruption is broadening beyond a single chokepoint print.
Structured disclosures and insurance commentary confirm the shipping shock is persisting rather than clearing quickly.
JPY basis is materially more negative.
EUR basis is also leaning into dollar scarcity.
Repo stress is building.
Treasury volatility is elevated.
Long-end auction demand is slipping.
Repeated weak 5Y/7Y coupon auctions show the belly is under strain.
Weak coupon auctions are clustering across recent cycles.
Basis trade stress is visible.
Foreign holders are drawing dollar liquidity.
Private credit marks are deteriorating.
Payroll growth is weakening as the shock reaches labor demand.
Temporary-help employment is deteriorating ahead of broader payroll weakness.
Direct spot FX and rate differentials confirm broader dollar funding strain.
Insurance-market fragmentation is extending the physical shock into a longer-lived regime transition.
Nuclear verification ambiguity is raising the odds of nonlinear repricing.
Sustained defensive burn-rate pressure raises escalation convexity.
Fragmented command signals reduce confidence in a clean reopening path.
Forward inflation expectations are climbing as the shock migrates into policy credibility.
The 5Y belly is richening versus the front and long end, signaling embedded persistence.
Survey-market expectation divergence is widening into a nonlinear repricing risk.
The combined expectations block shows persistence broadening beyond spot oil.
Japan, Europe, and China are absorbing a compounded local-currency energy shock.
Structural current-account and import dependence make importer stress more likely to migrate into funding and demand channels.
Equity volatility is broadening the tightening impulse beyond rates and oil.
The real-income squeeze is increasing the chance that the oil shock migrates into broader demand and credit stress.
A weakening labor-income tax base shows the oil shock is starting to damage nominal growth capacity.
Receipts-sensitive market stress confirms the migration from inflation shock into fiscal strain.
Buffer depletion is making the oil shock more path dependent and harder to absorb.
Importer reserve-cover stress is increasing the chance that energy strain migrates into funding channels.
A heavier external-balance burden raises convexity in foreign demand for Treasuries.
Soft liability-side financing support suggests a weaker marginal foreign bid for duration.
Foreign-duration sponsorship is weakening alongside importer and auction stress.

break.description: Repeated weak auctions are impairing duration clearing.
Persistent belly weakness shows duration stress is broadening across core coupon issuance.
A cluster of weak coupon auctions raises the probability that clearing stress becomes systemic.
Repo stress is persistent.
Foreign official holders are pulling dollars against Treasuries.
Fed swap lines are picking up.
Treasury depth is deteriorating.
Basis unwind pressure is visible.
Rates volatility is consistent with dysfunction.
Broader inflation inputs are leaking into credit.
Private credit is under markdown pressure.
Manual intervention headline flag is set.
Labor-market slack is rising and feeding household credit stress.
Payroll hiring is weakening, damaging withholding and growth confidence.
Hours worked are rolling over before broader job cuts fully show up.
The labor-income tax base is eroding, worsening fiscal strain.
Federal receipts quality is weakening, tightening the sovereign funding trap.
White-collar-sensitive temporary employment is signaling credit spillover risk.
Synthetic funding pressure remains elevated alongside broader plumbing stress.
Insurance cancellation and case-by-case write-back stress reinforce trade and funding dysfunction.
A widening verification gap creates abrupt tail-risk repricing pressure.
High interceptor burn rates raise the probability of forced escalation into a market break.
Governance fragmentation lowers the odds that chokepoint and war-risk stress can be resolved quickly.
Medium-term inflation expectations are high enough to constrain clean policy easing.
The full expected-inflation curve is inconsistent with a temporary commodity shock only.
Entrenched expectations raise repression risk once market plumbing is under strain.
Foreign importer balance-sheet stress is raising the odds of unstable marginal UST demand and Fed-plumbing pressure.
Structural importer fragility raises the odds that the external shock converts into unstable UST demand and fiscal-policy constraint.
Volatility, weaker equities, and softer receipts quality are worsening capital-gains-sensitive fiscal pressure.
High equity volatility is consistent with a broader risk-premium shock and tighter financial conditions.
Depleted oil buffers reduce the odds that the physical shock self-heals before financial stress bites.
Importer reserve-cover strain is consistent with unstable external demand for safe-duration assets.
The external financing burden is large enough that weaker sponsorship can amplify a Treasury break.
Weak BEA financing support points to less durable foreign absorption of new Treasury supply.
Foreign-duration sponsorship is deteriorating in a way that can destabilize sovereign clearing.
